narrative:
First Warn Headlines
Warm temperatures return today with highs climbing into the lower and middle 80s.
A cold front will move through this evening bringing a chance for scattered showers and an isolated thunderstorm.
The severe weather threat remains low, but a stronger storm cannot be completely ruled out.
Cooler and less humid air settles in Wednesday before another warming trend develops heading into the weekend.

Southwesterly winds are helping pump warmer air back into the bi-state region today as high pressure shifts east of the Mississippi Valley. After several cooler mornings recently, today will feel much more like late spring with afternoon temperatures climbing roughly five degrees warmer than Monday. Most locations across the St. Louis Metro, Central Missouri, Southeast Missouri, and Southern Illinois should top out in the lower to middle 80s under a mostly sunny sky.

Moisture levels will also slowly increase through the afternoon as dew points rise into the 50s. That added moisture combined with daytime heating may create enough instability for a few isolated storms to attempt development, especially across portions of Central and Northeast Missouri. Wind shear values are supportive of organized storms if one were to develop, but warmer air aloft is expected to act like a “lid” on the atmosphere and suppress widespread thunderstorm formation. Because of that, the severe weather threat remains low overall.

Later this evening and tonight, a cold front will slide southeast across the region bringing scattered showers and perhaps an isolated thunderstorm. Rain coverage now appears less widespread than earlier forecasts suggested, and many communities may only see brief passing rainfall. Still, don’t be surprised if you hear a rumble of thunder while trying to enjoy dinner outside. Mother Nature enjoys reminding us she still runs the schedule.

Behind the front, northerly winds return Wednesday with temperatures dropping back into the 70s along with lower humidity. The cooler air will be brief though, as another stronger warming trend develops heading toward the weekend. Forecast guidance continues to hint at summerlike warmth returning with temperatures potentially pushing back into the mid and upper 80s by Friday into the weekend along with periodic thunderstorm chances.
